violations.text: Beans were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 01/22/2026

Dishwasher located in the bar area was not in good repair. Per interview with PIC, unit is not in use. Establishment uses 3 bay sink or main dishwasher in kitchen area. Inspector observed an "out of order" sign on bar dishwasher. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2026

Raw animal foods (such as chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as produce and cheese) in the prep-unit refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC relocated raw chicken. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the bar dishwasher was 0 parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. PIC took unit out of service until more sanitizer is purchased. PIC will use 3 bay sink or kitchen dishwasher to wash/sanitize barware. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026

Bolognese sauce, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026

Chicken and cheese were observed held between 45-59 degrees Fahrenheit stored above prep unit items. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC relocated items to cooler for active cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. PIC labeled. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026

Chemicals were stored on the same shelf with food/equipment/single service articles in the kitchen and front service area. PIC relocated chemicals. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Soup was observed cooling on the stove top. PIC placed item in ice bath. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Bolognese sauce was placed the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep with lid and was still 48F the next day. Lid contained condensation. PIC discarded sauce. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026

The door to the outside, located in the front, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/27/2025

Knives were stored on an unclean surface (in prep unit) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. PIC washed/sanitized knives. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the kitchen dish machine. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the floor mixer have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/08/2026

Raw animal foods (such as raw ground beef) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as cooked soup) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC relocated raw beef. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, slicer, ice machine, immersion blender) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC took items out of service to be washed/sanitized. PIC disposed of ice. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2025
The consumer advisory provided does not disclose the foods on the menu that are raw or undercooked. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. PIC corrected on site. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2025

The door to the outside, located in the front, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 11/27/2025
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. PIC removed and washed/sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2025
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine in the kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the mixer shield have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2025
Inspector observed a food prep sink with an air break where an air gap is needed. New Violation. Correct By: 11/27/2025

Employees' personal belongings were observed stored in the pizza prep area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 11/13/2025
